description: You're well on your way to building a personal process that gets you started quickly and with limited friction.

title: Exercise 25: xargs
description: We are returning to challenge mode exercises and to warm you up you'll be implementing `xargs`.

title: Exercise 26: hexdump
description: You've done a warmup with `xargs` and are now working in the cycle of code then audit.

title: Exercise 27: tr
description: This exercise is continuing the study of doing TDD (aka Test First) style development.

title: Exercise 28: sh
description: You are now going to continue your TDD style process, but you'll add a small hack session to start.

title: Exercise 29: diff and patch
description: To finish [Part IV](part3.

title: Part IV: Intermediate Projects
description: In [Part III](part2.

title: Exercise 30: Finite State Machines
description: Whenever you read a book on parsing there's this scary chapter on Finite State Machines (FSM).

title: Exercise 31: Regular Expressions
description: A regular expression (regex) is a succinct way to encode how a sequence of characters should be matched in a string.

title: Exercise 32: Scanners
description: My first book very casually covered scanners in [Learn Python The Hard Way, Exercise 48](https://learnpythonthehardway.

title: Exercise 33: Parsers
description: Imagine you're given a huge list of numbers and you have to enter them into a spreadsheet.

title: Exercise 34: Analyzers
description: You now have a parser that should be producing a tree of grammar production objects.

title: Exercise 35: Interpreters
description: This final exercise in parsing should be both challenging and fun.

title: Exercise 36: Simple Calculator
description: This challenge is to create a simple algebraic calculator using everything you've learned about parsing.

title: Exercise 37: Little BASIC
description: You are now going to take a trip back in time to my childhood and implement a BASIC interpreter.

title: Part V: Parsing Text
description: This part of the book will teach you about text processing and, specifically, the beginning of parsing text formally.

title: Exercise 38: Introduction To SQL
description: The best way to learn how to model and design solid data is to start with the very basic building blocks.

title: Exercise 39: Creating with SQL
description: When we talk about the acronym "CRUD" the 'C' stands for "Create" and it doesn't just mean creating tables.

title: Exercise 40: Reading with SQL
description: Out of the CRUD matrix you only know "Create".

title: Exercise 41: Updating with SQL
description: You now know the CR parts of CRUD, which leaves the Update and Delete operations.

title: Exercise 42: Deleting with SQL
description: This is the simplest exercise, but I want you to think for a second before typing the code in.

title: Exercise 43: SQL Administration
description: The word "administration" is overloaded in databases.

title: Exercise 44: Using Python's Database API
description: Python has a standardized database API that enables you to use the same code to access multiple databases.

title: Exercise 45: Creating an ORM
description: The final exercise in the SQL part of the book is a big jump.

title: Part VI: SQL and Object Relational Mapping
description: In this part of the book we're going to cover something that doesn't quite fit into the rest of the book's structure, but is a very necessary topic for junior developers to understand.

title: Exercise 46: blog
description: You should have your process theme written up as described in the beginning of this section, and you should have your process listed out and ready to go.

title: Exercise 47: bc
description: You should be warmed up and ready to work on this new project.

title: Exercise 48: ed
description: If your process is working then you should be able to focus on one long project for a few weeks at a time.

title: Exercise 49: sed
description: You implemented a "baby version" of `sed` in exercise 9 when you were learning how to make quick and dirty hacks.

title: Exercise 50: vi
description: I am going to prison for this exercise.

title: Exercise 51: lessweb
description: We are nearing the end of the book, so I'm going to give you one project in the final two exercises.

title: Exercise 52: moreweb
description: Now that you've created a web server using the Python `http.

title: Part VII: Final Projects
description: The final part of the book is where you step up to more advanced projects and try to nail down your personal process.
